sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Fatigue for the past two months.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports fatigue for the past two months. The patient denies blood in stool. The patient's mom and her sister both had melanoma. The patient's grandfather had pancreatic cancer. The patient reports no personal history of cancerous or precancerous skin spots. The patient is allergic to augmentin. The patient owns an auto repair shop. The patient admits to smoking from time to time. The patient had gall bladder and appendix removed.
Findings
The doctor observed a one centimeter shave biopsy site with no residual pigmentation. The doctor palpated a slightly abnormal right groin lymph node. The pathology report indicates a malignant melanoma with a Breslow thickness of 0.7 millimeters. The pathology report indicates a Clark level 4. The pathology report indicates a stage 1 melanoma.
Assessment
Malignant melanoma, stage 1.
Plan
The doctor recommends a wide local excision. The doctor recommends regular follow-up with the dermatologist. The doctor recommends sunscreen use. Arrange follow-up with the dermatologist in two weeks post-excision. Order an ultrasound of the right groin lymph node.
